“Is not HWHY your Elohim with you? And has He not given you rest all around? For He has given the inhabitants of the land into my hand and the land has been subdued before HWHY and before His people.

“Now, give your heart and your HWHY being to seek your Elohim. Rise up and build the set-apart place of HWHY Elohim, to bring the ark of the covenant of HWHY and the set-apart vessels of Elohim into the house that is to be built for the Name of HWHY.”
Dawiḏ was old and satisfied with days and he made his son Shelomoh to reign over Yisra’ĕl,
and he gathered all the rulers of Yisra’ĕl with the priests and the Lĕwites.
And the Lĕwites were numbered from the age of thirty years and above. And their number, head by head, was thirty- eight thousand males.
Of these, twenty-four thousand were to oversee the work of the House of HWHY and six thousand were officers and judges.
Four thousand were gatekeepers and four thousand to give praise to HWHY, “which I made for giving praise.”
And Dawiḏ divided them into divisions, of the sons of Lĕwi: of Gĕreshon, Qehath and Merari.
Of the Gĕreshonites: La‛dan and Shim‛i.
The sons of La‛dan: Yeḥi’ĕl the head, then Zĕtham and Yo’ĕl, three.
The sons of Shim‛i: Shelomith and Ḥazi’ĕl and Haran, three. These were the heads of the fathers’ houses of La‛dan.
And the sons of Shim‛i: Yaḥath, Zina 961
And Yaḥath was the head and Zizah the second. But Ye‛ush and Beri‛ah did not have many sons, so they were reckoned as one father’s house.
The sons of Qehath: Amram, Yitshar, Ḥeḇron and Uzzi’ĕl, four.

The sons of Amram: Aharon and Mosheh. And Aharon was set-apart, he and his sons forever, that he should set- apart the most set-apart, to burn incense before HWHY, to serve Him and to give the blessing in His Name forever.
Now Mosheh, the man of Elohim, his sons were named after the tribe of Lĕwi.
The sons of Mosheh: Gĕreshom and Eli‛ezer.
The sons of Gĕreshom: Sheḇu’ĕl was the head.
And the sons of Eli‛ezer: Reḥaḇyah was the head. And Eli‛ezer had no other sons, but the sons of Reḥaḇyah were very many.
The sons of Yitshar: Shelomith was the head.
The sons of Ḥeḇron: Yeriyahu was the head, Amaryah the second, Yaḥazi’ĕl the third and Yeqam‛am the fourth.
The sons of Uzzi’ĕl: Miḵah was the head and Yishshiyah the second.
The sons of Merari: Maḥli and Mushi. The sons of Maḥli: El‛azar and Qish.
And El‛azar died and had no sons, but only daughters. And their brothers, the sons of Qish, took them.
The sons of Mushi: Maḥli, Ĕḏer and Yerĕmoth, three.

These were the sons of Lĕwi by their fathers’ houses, the heads of the fathers’ houses as they were counted head by head by the number of their names, who did the work for the service of the House of HWHY, from the age of twenty years and above.
For Dawiḏ said, “HWHY Elohim of Yisra’ĕl has given rest to His people and He dwells in Yerushalayim forever,”
and also of the Lĕwites, “They shall no longer bear the Dwelling Place, or any of the vessels for its service.”
For by the last words of Dawiḏ the Lĕwites were numbered from twenty years old and above,

because their duty was to help the sons of Aharon in the service of the House of HWHY, in the courtyards and in the rooms, in the cleansing of all that was set-apart and the work of the service of the House of Elohim,
both with the showbread and the fine flour for the grain offering and with the unleavened cakes and what is baked on the griddle and with what is mixed and with all kinds of measures and sizes;
and to stand every morning to thank and praise HWHY and so at evening;
and for all the burnt offerings to HWHY on the Sabbaths and on the New Moons and on the appointed times, by number according to the right-ruling upon them, continually before HWHY;
and that they should guard the duty of the Tent of Meeting, the duty of the set-apart place and the duty of the sons of Aharon their brothers in the service of HWHY. the House of
And the divisions of the sons of Aharon: The sons of Aharon were Naḏaḇ and Aḇihu, El‛azar and Ithamar.
But Naḏaḇ and Aḇihu died before their father and had no children, so El‛azar and Ithamar served as priests.
And Dawiḏ, with Tsaḏoq of the sons of El‛azar and Aḥimeleḵ of the sons of Ithamar, divided them according to their offices in their service.

And there were more leaders found of the sons of El‛azar than of the sons of Ithamar. So they divided the sons of El‛azar into sixteen heads of their fathers’ houses and the sons of Ithamar into eight heads of their fathers’ houses.
And they were divided by lot, one group as another, for there were officials of the set-apart place and officials of Elohim, from the sons of El‛azar and from the sons of Ithamar.
The scribe, Shemayah son of Nethanĕ’l, a Lĕwite, wrote them down
And the first lot came forth to Yehoyariḇ, the second to Yeḏayah,
the third to Ḥarim, the fourth to Se‛orim,
the fifth to Malkiyah, the sixth to Miyamin,
the seventh to Haqqots, the eighth to Aḇiyah,
the ninth to Yĕshua, the tenth to Sheḵanyahu,
the eleventh to Elyashiḇ, the twelfth to Yaqim,
the thirteenth to Ḥuppah, the fourteenth to Yesheḇ’aḇ,
the fifteenth to Bilgah, the sixteenth to Immĕr,
the seventeenth to Ḥĕzir, the eighteenth to Happitstsĕts,
the nineteenth to Pethaḥyah, the twentieth to Yeḥezqĕl,
the twenty-first to Yaḵin, the twentysecond to Gamul,
the twenty-third to Delayahu, the twenty-fourth to Ma‛azyahu.
These were their offices in their service for coming into the House of HWHY according to their right-ruling by the hand of Aharon their father, as HWHY Elohim of Yisra’ĕl had commanded him.
And the rest of the sons of Lĕwi: of the sons of Amram, Shuḇa’ĕl; of the sons of Shuḇa’ĕl, Yeḥdeyahu.
Concerning Reḥaḇyahu, of the sons of Reḥaḇyahu, the head was Yishshiyah.
Of the Yitsharites, Shelomoth; of the sons of Shelomoth, Yaḥath.
Of the sons of ÀeÄron, Yeriyahu was the head, Amaryahu the second, Yaḥazi’ĕl the third and Yeqam‛am the fourth.
Of the sons of Uzzi’ĕl, Miḵah; of the sons of Miḵah, Shamir.
The brother of Miḵah, Yishshiyah; of the sons of Yishshiyah, Zeḵaryahu.
The sons of Merari: Maḥli and Mushi; the son of Ya‛aziyahu, Beno.
The sons of Merari by Ya‛aziyahu: Beno and Shoham and Zakkur and Iḇri.
Of Maḥli: El‛azar, who had no sons.
Of Qish: the son of Qish, Yeraḥme’ĕl.
And the sons of Mushi: Maḥli, Ĕḏer and Yerimoth. These were the sons of the Lĕwites according to their fathers’ houses.

These also cast lots, they too, as their brothers the sons of Aharon, in the presence of Sovereign Dawiḏ and Tsaḏoq and Aḥimeleḵ and the heads of the fathers’ houses of the priests and Lĕwites – the head of the fathers, as well as his younger brothers.

And Dawiḏ and the commanders of the army separated for the service some of the sons of Asaph and of Hĕman and of Yeḏuthun, who should prophesy with lyres, with harps and with cymbals. And the number of the workmen according to their service was:
Of the sons of Asaph: Zakkur and Yosĕph, Nethanyah and Ashar’ĕlah, the sons of Asaph at the hands of Asaph,who prophesied at the hands of the sovereign.

Of Yeḏuthun, the sons of Yeḏuthun: Geḏalyahu and Tseri and Yeshayahu, Ḥashaḇyahu and Mattithyahu, six, under the hands of their father Yeḏuthun, who prophesied with a lyre to give thanks HWHY. and to praise
Of Hĕman, the sons of Hĕman: Buqqiyahu, Mattanyahu, Uzzi’ĕl, Sheḇu’ĕl, Yerimoth, Ḥananyah, Ḥanani, Eliyathah, Giddalti and Romamti-Ezer, Yoshbeqashah, Mallothi, Hothir, Maḥaziyoth.
All these were the sons of Hĕman the sovereign’s seer in the words of Elohim, to exalt his horn. For Elohim gave Hĕman fourteen sons and three daughters.
All these were at the hands of their father for song in the House of HWHY, with cymbals, harps and lyres, for the 963
And the number of them, with their brothers who were taught in the songs of HWHY, all who were skilled, was two hundred and eighty-eight.
And they cast lots for their duty, the small as well as the great, the teacher with the student.
And the first lot for Asaph came forth for Yosĕph; Geḏalyahu the second, he and his brothers and sons, twelve;
the third Zakkur, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the fourth Yitsri, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the fifth Nethanyahu, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the sixth Buqqiyahu, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the seventh Yesar’ĕlah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the eighth Yeshayahu, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the ninth Mattanyahu, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the tenth Shim‛i, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the eleventh Azar’ĕl, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the twelfth Ḥashaḇyah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the thirteenth Shuḇa’ĕl, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the fourteenth Mattithyahu, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the fifteenth Yerĕmoth, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the sixteenth Ḥananyahu, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the seventeenth Yoshbeqashah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the eighteenth Ḥanani, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the nineteenth Mallothi, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the twentieth Eliyathah, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the twenty-first Hothir, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the twenty-second Giddalti, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the twenty-third Maḥaziyoth, his sons and his brothers, twelve;
the twenty-fourth Romamti-Ezer, his sons and his brothers, twelve.
For the divisions of the gatekeepers: Of the Qorḥites, Meshelemyahu son of Qorĕ, of the sons of Asaph.
And the sons of Meshelemyahu: Zeḵaryahu the first-born, Yeḏiya’ĕl the second, Zeḇaḏyahu the third, Yathni’ĕl the fourth,
Ĕylam the fifth, Yehoḥanan the sixth, Elyo‛ĕynai the seventh.
And the sons of Oḇĕḏ-Eḏom: Shemayah the first-born, Yehozaḇaḏ the second, Yo’aḥ the third, Saḵar the fourth, Nethanĕ’l the fifth,
Ammi’ĕl the sixth, Yissasḵar the seventh, Pe‛ullethai the eighth, for Elohim blessed him.
Also to Shemayah his son were sons born who ruled their fathers’ houses, because they were able mighty men.
The sons of Shemayah: Othni and Repha’ĕl and Oḇĕḏ, Elzaḇaḏ, whose brothers Elihu and Semaḵyahu were able men.
All these were of the sons of Oḇĕḏ Eḏom, they and their sons and their brothers, able men with strength for the work: sixty-two of Oḇĕḏ-Eḏom.
And Meshelemyahu had sons and brothers, eighteen able men.
Also Ḥosah, of the children of Merari, had sons: Shimri the head (although he was not the first-born, his father made him the head),
Ḥilqiyahu the second, Teḇalyahu the third, Zeḵaryahu the fourth. All the sons and brothers of Ḥosah were thirteen.
These divisions of the gatekeepers, among the chief men, had duties like their brothers, to serve in the House of HWHY.
And they cast lots for each gate, the small as well as the great, according to 964
And the lot to the east fell to Shelemyahu. Then they cast lots for his son Zeḵaryahu, a wise counsellor and his lot came out to the north;
to Oḇĕḏ-Eḏom to the south and to his sons the storehouse;
to Shuppim and Ḥosah to the west, with the Shalleḵeth Gate on the ascending highway; guard corresponding with guard.
On the east were six Lĕwites, on the north four each day, on the south four each day and for the storehouse two by two.
As for the Parbar on the west, there were four on the highway and two at the Parbar.
These were the divisions of the gatekeepers among the sons of Qoraḥ and among the sons of Merari.
Of the Lĕwites, Aḥiyah was over the treasuries of the House of Elohim and over the treasuries of the set-apart gifts.
The sons of La‛dan, the sons of the Gĕreshonites of La‛dan, heads of their fathers’ houses, of La‛dan the Gĕreshonite: Yeḥi’ĕli.
The sons of Yeḥi’ĕli, Zĕtham and Yo’ĕl his brother, were over the HWHY. treasuries of the House of
Of the Amramites, the Yitsharites, the Ḥeḇronites and the Uzzi’ĕlites,
even Sheḇu’ĕl son of Gĕreshom, son of Mosheh, was overseer of the treasuries.
And his brothers by Eli‛ezer were Reḥaḇyahu his son and Yeshayahu his son and Yoram his son and Ziḵri his son and Shelomoth his son.

This Shelomoth and his brothers were over all the treasuries of the set-apart gifts which Sovereign Dawiḏ and the heads of fathers’ houses, the commanders over thousands and hundreds and the commanders of the army, had set-apart.
Some of the booty won in battles they set-apart to maintain the House of HWHY.
And all that Shemu’ĕl the seer and Sha’ul son of Qish and Aḇnĕr son of Nĕr and Yo’aḇ son of Tseruyah had set-apart, all who were setting apart, were under the hand of Shelomith and his brothers.
Of the Yitsharites, Kenanyahu and his sons were for the outward duties as officials and judges over Yisra’ĕl.

Of the Ḥeḇronites, Ḥashaḇyahu and his brothers, one thousand seven hundred able men, had the oversight of Yisra’ĕl beyond the Yardĕn westward, for all the work of HWHY and in the service of the sovereign.

Of the Ḥeḇronites, Yeriyah was head of the Ḥeḇronites according to his genealogy of the fathers. In the fortieth year of the reign of Dawiḏ they were sought and there were found among them able men at Ya‛zĕr of Gil‛aḏ.

And his brothers were two thousand seven hundred able men, heads of fathers’ houses, whom Sovereign Dawiḏ made officials over the Re’uḇĕnites and the Gaḏites and the half-tribe of Menashsheh, in all matters of Elohim and matters of the sovereign.

And the children of Yisra’ĕl, according to their number, the heads of fathers’ houses and the commanders of thousands and hundreds and their officers, served the sovereign in all matters of the divisions which came in and went out month by month through- out all the months of the year, each division having twenty-four thousand.
Over the first division for the first month was Yashoḇ‛am son of Zaḇdi’ĕl and in his division were twenty-four thousand;
of the sons of Perets and the chief of all the commanders of the army for the first month.
And over the division of the second month was Doḏai an Aḥoḥite and of his division Miqloth also was the leader and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
The third commander of the army 965
This was the Benayahu who was mighty among the thirty and was over the thirty and in his division was Ammizaḇaḏ his son.
The fourth for the fourth month was Asah’ĕl the brother of Yo’aḇ and Zeḇaḏyah his son after him and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
The fifth for the fifth month was Shamhuth the Yizraḥite and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
The sixth for the sixth month was Ira son of Iqqĕsh the Teqowite and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
The seventh for the seventh month was Ḥĕlets the Pelonite, of the children of Ephrayim and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
The eighth for the eighth month was Sibbeḵai the Ḥushathite, of the Zarḥites and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
The ninth for the ninth month was Aḇi‛ezer the Anathothite, of the Binyamites and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
The tenth for the tenth month was Maharai the Netophathite, of the Zarḥites and in his division were twenty- four thousand.
The eleventh for the eleventh month was Benayah the Pirathonite, of the children of Ephrayim and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
The twelfth for the twelfth month was Ḥeldai the Netophathite, of Othni’ĕl and in his division were twenty-four thousand.
And over the tribes of Yisra’ĕl: the chief officer over Re’uḇĕn: Eli‛ezer son of Ziḵri; over Shim‛on: Shephatyahu son of Ma‛aḵah;
Lĕwi: Ḥashaḇyah son of Qemu’ĕl; over Aharon: Tsaḏoq;
Yahuḏah: Elihu, one of Dawiḏ’s brothers; Yissasḵar: Omri son of Miḵa’ĕl;
Zeḇulun: Yishmayahu son of Oḇaḏyahu; Naphtali: Yerimoth son of Azri’ĕl;
the children of Ephrayim: Hoshĕa son of Azazyahu; the half-tribe of Menashsheh: Yo’ĕl son of Peḏayahu;
the half-tribe of Menashsheh in Gil‛aḏ: Iddo son of Zeḵaryahu; Binyamin: Ya‛asi’ĕl son of Aḇnĕr;
Dan: Azar’ĕl son of Yeroḥam. These were the rulers of the tribes of Yisra’ĕl.
But Dawiḏ did not take the number of those twenty years old and under, HWHY because had said He would increase Yisra’ĕl like the stars of the heavens.
Yo’aḇ son of Tseruyah began a census but he did not finish, for wrath came upon Yisra’ĕl because of this census; nor was the number recorded in the annals of Sovereign Dawiḏ.
And Azmaweth son of Aḏi’ĕl was over the sovereign’s treasuries. And Yehonathan son of Uzziyahu was over the store houses in the field, in the cities and in the villages and in the watchtowers.
And Ezri son of Keluḇ was over those who did the work of the field for tiling the ground.
And Shim‛i the Ramathite was over the vineyards and Zaḇdi the Shiphmite was over the increase of the vineyards for the wine cellars.
And Ba‛al-Ḥanan the Geḏĕrite was over the olive trees and the sycamore trees that were in the low country and Yo‛ash was over the oil- stores.
And Shitrai the Sharonite was over the herds that fed in Sharon and Shaphat son of Aḏlai was over the herds that were in the valleys.
And Oḇil the Yishma‛ĕlite was over the camels and Yeḥdeyahu the Mĕronothite was over the donkeys,
and Yaziz the Haḡrite was over the flocks. All of these were heads over the property of Sovereign Dawiḏ.
Also Yehonathan, Dawiḏ’s uncle, was a counsellor, a wise man and a scribe. And Yeḥi’ĕl son of Ḥaḵmoni was with
And Aḥithophel was the sovereign’s counsellor and Ḥushai the Arkite was the sovereign’s companion.
And after Aḥithophel was Yehoyaḏa son of Benayahu, then Eḇyathar. And the chief of the sovereign’s army was Yo’aḇ.

And Dawiḏ assembled at Yerushalayim all the rulers of Yisra’ĕl: the rulers of the tribes and the commanders of the divisions who served the sovereign, the commanders over thousands and commanders over hundreds and the heads over all the property and possessions of the sovereign and of his sons, with the officials, the mighty men and all the mighty brave men.

Then Sovereign Dawiḏ rose to his feet and said, “Hear me, my brothers and my people: I had it in my heart to build a house of rest for the ark of the covenant of HWHY and for the footstool of our Elohim and had made preparations to build it.
“But Elohim said to me, ‘You do not build a house for My Name, because you have been a man of battle and have shed blood.’

“Yet HWHY Elohim of Yisra’ĕl chose me above all the house of my father to be sovereign over Yisra’ĕl forever, for He has chosen Yahuḏah to be the ruler. And of the house of Yahuḏah, the house of my father and among the sons of my father, He was pleased with me to make me reign over all Yisra’ĕl. HWHY
“And of all my sons – for has given me many sons – He has chosen my son Shelomoh to sit on the throne of the reign of HWHY over Yisra’ĕl,
and said to me, ‘Shelomoh your son is the one to build My house and My courtyards, for I have chosen him to be My son and I Myself am a Father to him.
‘And I shall establish his reign forever, if he is strong to do My commands and My right-rulings, as it is this day.’

“And now, before the eyes of all Yisra’ĕl, the assembly of HWHY and in the hearing of our Elohim, guard and seek all the commands of HWHY your Elohim, so that you possess this good land and leave it as an inheritance for your children after you forever.

“As for you, my son Shelomoh, know the Elohim of your father and serve Him with a perfect heart and with a pleasing life, for HWHY searches all hearts and understands all the intent of the thoughts. If you do seek Him, He is found by you; but if you forsake Him, He rejects you forever.
“See, now, for HWHY has chosen you to build a house for the set-apart place. Be strong and do it.”
And Dawiḏ gave his son Shelomoh the plans for the porch and its houses and its treasuries and its upper rooms and its inner rooms and the place of atonement;

and the plans for all that he had by the Spirit, of the courtyards of the House of HWHY and of all the rooms all around and of the treasuries of the House of Elohim and of the treasuries for the set- apart gifts;
and for the divisions of the priests and the Lĕwites and for all the work of the HWHy service of the House of and for all the vessels of service in the House of HWHY;
even gold by weight for utensils of gold, for all utensils used in every kind of service; for silver for all utensils of silver by weight, for all utensils used in every kind of service;

and by weight for the lampstands of gold and their lamps of gold, by weight for each lampstand and its lamps; and for the lampstands of silver by weight, for the lampstand and its lamps, according to the use of each lampstand;
and by weight gold for the tables of the showbread, for each table and silver for the tables of silver;
and clean gold for the forks and the 967
and refined gold by weight for the altar of incense and for the pattern of the chariot of the gold keruḥim, spreading out their wings and covering over the ark of the covenant of HWHY.
“HWHY made me understand all this in writing, by His hand upon me, all the works of these plans.”

And Dawiḏ said to his son Shelomoh, “Be strong and courageous and do it. HWHY Do not fear nor be afraid, for Elohim, my Elohim, is with you. He shall not leave you nor forsake you, until you have completed all the work HWHY. for the service of the House of

“And see, the divisions of the priests and the Lĕwites for all the service of the House of Elohim and every volunteer of any skill is with you in all work, for every kind of service. And the rulers and all the people are entirely at your command.”
And Dawiḏ the sovereign said to all the assembly, “My son Shelomoh, the one whom Elohim has chosen, is young and tender. And the work is great, because the palace is not for man HWHY but for Elohim.

“And with all my power I have pre pared for the House of my Elohim: the gold for the gold and the silver for the silver and the bronze for the bronze, the iron for the iron and the wood for the wood, shoham stones and settings and fair stones of various colours and all kinds of precious stones and marble slabs – in large quantities.
“And also, because I delighted in the House of my Elohim, I have treasure of gold and silver; I give for the House of my Elohim even more than all that I have prepared for the Set-apart House:
three thousand talents of gold, of the gold of Ophir and seven thousand talents of refined silver, to overlay the walls of the houses;
the gold for the gold and the silver for the silver and for all kinds of work to be done by the hands of craftsmen. Who then is moved to fill his hand today for HWHY?”
And the leaders of the fathers’ houses and the leaders of the tribes of Yisra’ĕl and the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, with the heads over the sovereign’s work, volunteered

and gave for the work of the House of Elohim five thousand talents and ten thousand darics of gold and ten thousand talents of silver and eighteen thousand talents of bronze and one hundred thousand talents of iron.
And those with whom were found precious stones gave them to the treasury of the House of HWHY, into the hand of Yeḥi’ĕl the Gĕreshonite.
And the people rejoiced, for they had given voluntarily, because with a perfect heart they had given voluntarily to HWHY. And Dawiḏ the sovereign also rejoiced greatly.
And Dawiḏ blessed HWHY before all the assembly. And Dawiḏ said, “Blessed are You, HWHY Elohim of Yisra’ĕl, our Father, forever and ever.

“Yours, O HWHY, is the greatness, the power and the comeliness, the preeminence and the excellency, because of all that is in the heavens and in the earth. Yours is the reign, O HWHY and You are exalted as head above all.
“And the riches and the esteem come from Your presence and You rule over all. And in Your hand is power and might and it is in Your hand to make great and to give strength to all.
“And now, our Elohim, we thank You and praise Your comely Name.
“But who am I and who are my people, that we should be able to give so voluntarily as this? For all comes from You and we have given to You out of Your hand.
“For we are sojourners and pilgrims before You, as were all our fathers; our days on earth are as a shadow and without permanence.
“O HWHY our Elohim, all this store that we have prepared to build You a house for Your set-apart Name is from Your hand and all is of You.

“And I know, my Elohim, that You are trying the heart and desire uprightness. As for me, in the uprightness of my heart I have voluntarily given all these. And now with joy I have seen Your people, who are present here to give voluntarily to You.
“O HWHY Elohim of Aḇrahim, Yitsḥaq and Yisra’ĕl, our fathers, guard this forever in the intent of the thoughts of the heart of Your people and prepare their heart toward You.
“And give my son Shelomoh a perfect heart to guard Your commands and Your witnesses and Your laws, to do all and to build the palace for which I have prepared.”
And Dawiḏ said to all the assembly, “Now bless HWHY your Elohim.” And all the assembly blessed HWHY Elohim of their fathers and bowed their HWHY heads and did obeisance to and the sovereign.

And they made offerings to HWHY HWHY and offered burnt offerings to on the next day: a thousand bulls, a thousand rams, a thousand lambs, with their drink offerings and slaughterings in large numbers for all Yisra’ĕl.
And they ate and drank before HWHY with great joy on that day. And they set up Shelomoh son of Dawiḏ to reign the second time, and anointed him before HWHY to be leader and Tsaḏoq to be priest.
And Shelomoh sat on the throne of HWHY as sovereign instead of Dawiḏ his father and prospered; and all Yisra’ĕl obeyed him.
And all the rulers and the mighty men and also all the sons of Sovereign Dawiḏ, submitted themselves to Shelomoh the sovereign.
And HWHY exalted Shelomoh exceedingly in the eyes of all Yisra’ĕl and put upon him such royal splendour as had not been on any sovereign before him in Yisra’ĕl.
Thus Dawiḏ son of Yishai reigned over all Yisra’ĕl.
And the days that he reigned over Yisra’ĕl was forty years. He reigned seven years in Ḥeḇron and he reigned thirty-three years in Yerushalayim.
And he died in a good old age, satisfied with days, riches and esteem. And Shelomoh his son reigned in his place.
And the acts of Sovereign Dawiḏ, first and last, see, they are written in the book of Shemu’ĕl the seer, in the book of Nathan the prophet and in the book of Gaḏ the seer,
with all his reign and his might and the times that passed over him and over Yisra’ĕl and over all the reigns of the lands.
